← All worlds/World 08
World 08 · 1 state

Grief

Films for the goodbyes you have to keep saying — to people, to versions of yourself, to lives you thought you were building.

Choose an emotion
Learning to Live Again
On Learning to Live Again

Nobody tells you that grief is not linear. They say it, sometimes — the stages, the model — but knowing it abstractly does not prepare you for Tuesday, three months later, when you are fine in the morning and then a song comes on in a grocery store and you have to leave the cart and sit in your car. Grief has no schedule. It has no graduation. The films here were not chosen to help you process. They were chosen because they understand that some loss you live alongside, not through — and they treat that truth with the honesty it deserves.

Learning to Live Again

Films for the slow return of weight to your feet.

Steel Magnolias

Herbert Ross · 1989

CS9.4
2016

Manchester by the Sea

Kenneth Lonergan

View Film →

A janitor returns to his hometown after his brother’s death and finds himself the guardian of his teenage nephew — carrying a past he cannot speak.

CS8.6
2017

A Ghost Story

David Lowery

View Film →

After a young musician dies, his ghost — a figure under a sheet — lingers in the house he shared with his wife, watching time pass without him.

CS8.9
2019

The Farewell

Lulu Wang

View Film →

A Chinese-American family flies home to Changchun to gather around their grandmother for what they know is a goodbye — except she doesn’t know she’s dying.

CS9.0
2017

Coco

Lee Unkrich

View Film →

A Mexican boy who dreams of being a musician finds himself in the Land of the Dead on Día de los Muertos and tries to find his way back — with the help of a great-great-grandfather.

CS8.2
2010

Rabbit Hole

John Cameron Mitchell

View Film →

Eight months after their young son’s accident, a couple in upstate New York drifts apart and toward different ways of trying to survive what happened.

CS9.2
1993

Three Colors: Blue

Krzysztof Kieślowski

View Film →

After her composer husband and young daughter die in a car crash, a Parisian woman tries to live as if she has no history, no name, and no need.

CS9.3
2021

Drive My Car

Ryusuke Hamaguchi

View Film →

A theatre director rehearsing a multilingual production of Uncle Vanya is paired with a quiet young driver — and slowly begins to speak about his wife.

CS9.5
2022

Aftersun

Charlotte Wells

View Film →

A father and his eleven-year-old daughter spend a summer holiday in Turkey — a memory revisited decades later by the woman she has become.

CS9.1
2016

Arrival

Denis Villeneuve

View Film →

A linguist is recruited to help communicate with newly-arrived alien visitors — and discovers that learning their language begins to bend her relationship with time.

CS8.5
2003

Big Fish

Tim Burton

View Film →

A son returns to his dying father’s bedside and finally listens to the tall tales he’s spent his life trying to disbelieve.

CS9.2
1957

Wild Strawberries

Ingmar Bergman

View Film →

An elderly professor driving to receive an honorary degree confronts the emotional failures of his life through dreams and memories.

CS8.9
1980

Ordinary People

Robert Redford

View Film →

A suburban Chicago family tries to function after a boating accident kills one son. The survivor and both parents are each separately failing.

CS8.4
2011

The Descendants

Alexander Payne

View Film →

A man in Hawaii confronts his wife’s impending death from a coma and the revelation that she was having an affair.

CS8.8
2010

Beginners

Mike Mills

View Film →

A son processes his father’s death after the man came out as gay at seventy-five and fell in love for the first time.

CS8.7
2015

45 Years

Andrew Haigh

View Film →

Five days before their forty-fifth anniversary party, a couple learns a body has been found — his girlfriend who died in an alpine accident fifty years ago.

CS9.1
1997

Sweet Hereafter

Atom Egoyan

View Film →

A school bus falls through ice in a small Canadian town, killing most of the children. A lawyer arrives to organize a lawsuit.

CS8.3
2014

Still Alice

Glatzer & Westmoreland

View Film →

A linguistics professor is diagnosed with early-onset Alzheimer’s. The film follows her and her family through the disease.

CS8.6
2016

A Monster Calls

J.A. Bayona

View Film →

A boy whose mother is dying is visited by a monster who tells him three stories and demands a fourth — the boy’s own truth.

CS8.3
2014

The Babadook

Jennifer Kent

View Film →

A single mother whose husband died in a car accident on the day their son was born begins to be tormented by a figure from a children’s book.

CS8.1
2007

In the Valley of Elah

Paul Haggis

View Film →

A retired military investigator searches for his soldier son after he goes missing following a return from Iraq.

CS8.2
2010

Rabbit Hole

John Cameron Mitchell

View Film →

Eight months after their young son’s death, a couple in upstate New York grieves in two entirely different directions.

CS8.9
2019

The Farewell

Lulu Wang

View Film →

A Chinese-American family gathers around their grandmother for a fake wedding, concealing her terminal diagnosis.

CS7.9
2019

Our Friend

Gabriela Cowperthwaite

View Film →

A friend moves in with a couple for a year and a half while the wife is dying of cancer. Based on Matthew Teague’s essay.

CS8.9
1980

Ordinary People

Robert Redford

View Film →

A suburban family navigates grief after a boating accident kills one son and leaves the other carrying the guilt of survival.

CS8.0
1989

Steel Magnolias

Herbert Ross

View Film →

Six women in a small Louisiana town support each other through a death in their group. The film is the friendship before and after.

Inner Peace →

For the quiet you’ve been earning.

Films don't just entertain. They quietly confirm you were seen — long before anyone in your real life knew how to.

18 emotional states·277 curated films·Growing weekly
